body.title: datacenter_mcp.py wrapper + .mcp.json entry — wire AnnounceMcpServer into Claude Code
body.result: datacenter_mcp.py wrapper + .mcp.json entry ship: 3 MCP tools (announce/manifest/check_for_invalidate) backed by lazy AnnounceMcpServer singleton. JSON TextContent serialization. 7 wrapper tests + manual import smoke. TheIgors commit a0459af7.
body.description:
AnnounceMcpServer ships as a Python class (slice 3b) but Claude Code can't invoke it until we provide an MCP stdio entry point. This ticket adds:
  1. lab/claudecode/datacenter_mcp.py — thin MCP stdio wrapper that imports AnnounceMcpServer and registers its three methods (announce / manifest / check_for_invalidate) as MCP tools
  2. .mcp.json entry naming the new server so CC sessions auto-discover it

**Affected files:** /home/akien/TheIgors/lab/claudecode/datacenter_mcp.py (NEW — modeled on igor_mcp.py), /home/akien/TheIgors/.mcp.json (add 'datacenter' server entry), /home/akien/TheIgors/tests/test_datacenter_mcp_wrapper.py (NEW — wrapper-level dispatch tests)
**Design rules:** matches igor_mcp.py's transport pattern (mcp.server.Server + stdio_server); singleton AnnounceMcpServer instantiated at module import (so tools share one client + cached manifest); tool methods return the JSON dicts AnnounceMcpServer already produces — no shape transformations.
**Scope boundary:** in — wrapper script, .mcp.json entry, tool-dispatch tests. Out — Igor-side reciprocal MCP tools, dynamic profile selection at runtime (cc.yaml is hardcoded), end-to-end CC integration test (requires live skeleton).
**Test plan:** test_wrapper_imports_without_error; test_wrapper_exposes_three_tools (lists declared MCP tools, asserts presence); test_announce_tool_dispatches_to_adapter (mock adapter, call wrapper tool, verify forwarding); test_manifest_tool_dispatches_to_adapter; test_check_for_invalidate_tool_dispatches_to_adapter.
